reportText: @ Canyon Mouth , 253 cfs @ Fort Collins , 1 cfs @ North Fork below Seaman Reservoir The water is on the rise! Clarity is changing daily. Be looking for slow water on the edges, seams, and inside bends. Dry-dropper rigs continue to be our bread and butter. As water levels rise, larger foam flies in sizes #6–10 are a great option to float some of the larger stonefly nymphs. Ants, Beetles, and Caddis will be your more productive dry fly fishing. Streamers are getting love also, think dark colors like black or olive with the darker water. We are getting more guide trips out on the Poudre every week. Give us a call to get the latest river report and current conditions! Take a look at our Poudre Assortment , a great selection for this time of year.
